Tub of Blood Bunch.description: An early New York waterfront street gang active in the late 1860s, operating from a bar called the Tub of Blood in the Corlears' Hook section of the East River. The gang included members such as Skinner Meehan, Dutch Hen, Jack Cody, Sweeney the Boy, Brian Boru, and Hop Along Peter. They dominated the waterfront and engaged in robberies. The gang faded into obscurity after the 1880s.

Lynx gang.description: The Lynx gang was a Birmingham-based gang originally composed of British Pakistani members, founded in the 1970s in the Sparkhill area. It was initially formed to protect the British Pakistani community, particularly Mirpuri immigrants, from White power skinheads. The gang is now defunct.

edges.evidence_quote: They were close to criminals in the English Italian Sabini family. However, the two families became rivals because the Cortesi brothers believed they were being denied a fair share of the proceeds from bookmaking and West End gambling clubs.

'Ndrina Arena.description: The 'Ndrina Arena is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Isola Capo Rizzuto, Calabria, Italy. It has influence in the Catanzaro area through alliances with local clans. The organization is involved in drug and arms trafficking, extortion, counterfeiting, and auto theft, with international operations in Brazil and Germany. Key figures include Nicola Arena and Carmine Arena.

descriptions: Arena is a subunit of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Arena è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese

Zwi Migdal.description: A Jewish criminal organization founded in Poland in the 1860s, based mainly in Argentina. It trafficked women from Central Europe into sexual slavery and forced prostitution, operating until 1939. At its height, it had 430 members overseeing 2,000 brothels and 4,000 women in Argentina alone.

descriptions: The infobox lists Zwi Migdal as an ally of Ashkenazum.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Zwi Migdal

descriptions: Ashkenazum was a splinter group that broke away from Zwi Migdal.
edges.evidence_quote: Ashkenazum was founded by Simon Rubinstein as a splinter group from the larger Zwi Migdal

descriptions: Ashkenazum is a splinter group that split from Zwi Migdal.
edges.evidence_quote: Zwi Migdal later split and a splinter group led by Simon Rubinstein established its own society named Ashkenazum.

descriptions: Zwi Migdal is allied with the Jewish Mob according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Jewish Mob , Ashkenazum

Cachiros.description: Honduran criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking, founded in the early 1990s by cattle farmers in Colón and Olancho. Leaders include Javier Maradiaga and Devis Leonel Rivera Maradiaga, who surrendered to the US in 2015 and testified about government complicity. The group's net worth exceeds one billion dollars.

Five Point Generalz.description: Street gang based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, founded in the late 1990s by five original members known as 'the five generals'. The gang operates primarily in the Mount Dennis neighborhood and is involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, and robbery. It gained national attention for its involvement in the 2005 Boxing Day shooting and other violent incidents. The gang remains active, with police raids in 2018 targeting its members.

descriptions: The two gangs were involved in a shootout that killed a bystander.
edges.evidence_quote: 11-year-old Ephraim Brown was killed by a stray bullet to the neck during a gang shootout, allegedly involving the Five Point Generalz and rival gang the Baghdad Crew

descriptions: G2M is a younger subset of the Five Point Generalz.
edges.evidence_quote: Toronto Police conducted a series of raids across the Greater Toronto Area targeting the Five Point Generalz and mainly the younger subset G2M.

descriptions: Dojin-kai is a member of the Yonsha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dojin-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi fraternal federation, the Yonsha-kai, formed with three other northern-Kyushu based independent yakuza syndicates, the Kudo-kai, Taishu-kai and Kumamoto-kai.

descriptions: Kudo-kai is a member of the Yonsha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kudo-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi fraternal federation, the Yonsha-kai, with three other northern-Kyushu based organizations, the Taishu-kai , Dojin-kai , and Kumamoto-kai.

Mali Boys.description: Organised street gang based in Leyton and Walthamstow, East London, controlling the local drug trade with extreme violence. Emerged in 2015 after splintering from the Beaumont Crew. Known for county lines drug networks and criminal child exploitation. Estimated 60-100 members as of 2019.

Galician Mafia.description: Drug smuggling groups based in the Spanish region of Galicia, originating from tobacco smuggling by local fishermen. They traffic cocaine, hashish, heroin, and illegal tobacco into Spain and Portugal, with connections to Colombian, Moroccan, and British criminal networks. Known for violent clan feuds and allegations of political corruption.

descriptions: The Galician Mafia is allied with 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Colombian cartels , Mexican drug cartels , British crime firms , French Milieu , Gang de la Brise de Mer , Camorra , Sicilian Mafia , 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: Galician clans have done business with the Albanian mafia regarding heroin.

descriptions: British crime firms work together with Galician clans to smuggle drugs into the UK.

Bandas y grupos emergentes en Colombia.description: Mafia organizations operating in Colombia, active in the internal armed conflict. They engage in homicide, extortion, kidnapping, forced recruitment, and drug trafficking. Formed after the demobilization of paramilitaries in 2006, they include groups like Clan del Golfo and FARC dissidents. They have alliances with Mexican cartels and operate across Colombia and neighboring countries.

edges.target: Bandas y grupos emergentes en Colombia
descriptions: The Cartel de los Soles is mentioned alongside BACRIM as operating in Venezuela, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: las bandas criminales de Colombia (BACRIM) ... operan en el país

edges.target: Bandas y grupos emergentes en Colombia
descriptions: BACRIM is a successor organization to the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: The AUC was supposed to have demobilized completely by 2006 but successor organizations continued to operate such as the neo-paramilitary alliance BACRIM ('bandas criminales emergentes').

descriptions: Hells Angels fought with Luton Town MIGs in a brawl.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Hells Angels' Lea Valley chapter took part in a mass brawl against a group from the Luton Town MIGs hooligan firm at the Blockers Arms public house in Luton in May 1990.

descriptions: Alternative founding theory ties Hells Angels to a member of Redlands Road Runners.
edges.evidence_quote: According to an alternative theory, the Hells Angels were founded on November 15, 1951, in San Bernardino, by Dick White, a member of the Redlands Road Runners.
